summary: Implement the artifact-independent OpenSCAD WebAssembly runtime core for validated virtual project staging, native-aligned CLI execution, output capture, result parsing, exports, and complete cleanup.
specSections: Architecture A-2 dedicated WebAssembly worker and virtual file system
FR-4.2 render, export, and version operations
FR-4.3 preview and full render semantics
FR-4.8 verbatim engine output and diagnostics
Appendix A normative request and result types

externalInputs.purpose: Inspect the generated official-source Emscripten module boundary and confirm noInitialRun/noExitRuntime options, byte output hooks, callMain, and the exposed virtual filesystem methods used by the fresh adapter.

decisions.decision: Decode only a generated-module default factory that resolves a complete callMain and filesystem surface, then implement render, export, and version behavior against that injected boundary so native-aligned VFS and CLI logic is exhaustively testable without executing or bundling GPL artifact bytes.
decisions.rationale: The generated JavaScript is a separately fetched engine artifact and must never be statically imported into the application bundle. An injected runtime core proves ScadMill-owned behavior independently, keeps the worker glue small, and leaves the real artifact, caching, progress, and license boundary for explicit integration gates.

decisions.topic: Private monotonic WASM workspaces and cleanup
decisions.decision: Stage every operation beneath a private monotonic /scadmill/jobs root, validate portable normalized layouts and a UTF-8 text entry before callMain, and recursively remove all partial or complete workspace nodes after success, CLI failure, parse failure, or staging failure while preserving the original error.
decisions.rationale: A reused Emscripten filesystem otherwise leaks assets between renders and can accumulate partial files after a failed write. Private roots plus fault-injected cleanup proof prevent stale-project influence and keep sequential worker reuse safe for the real integration test.

decisions.decision: Contain output-observer exceptions outside the engine lifecycle, flush unterminated streams by first-pending-byte order, and append an explicitly labeled ScadMill adapter error to prior verbatim engine output while adding a structured adapter diagnostic.
decisions.rationale: A UI observer must not reject an engine promise or skip VFS cleanup. Engine output must remain visible when a later artifact read or parse fails, and the adapter cause must be distinguishable from engine stderr rather than silently discarded or misattributed.

testEvidence.criterion: The injected runtime mirrors native staging, deterministic parameters, preview/full arguments, render fallback, output capture, parsing, exports, and ordinary cleanup
testEvidence.test: Run the focused fake-module and in-memory-filesystem runtime suite against behavioral stubs, then implement the complete pure runtime boundary.

red.outcome: RED: 1 test file failed and all 8 behavioral tests failed against the compilable stubs.

green.outcome: GREEN: 1 test file and all 8 tests passed in 505 ms after the first runtime implementation.

testEvidence.criterion: Version and PNG behavior are proven, multibyte mixed-stream output remains intact, and partial staging never leaves virtual filesystem residue
testEvidence.test: Add version outcome cases, exact PNG argument/refusal cases, interleaved UTF-8 output, and fault-injected mkdir/write failures that assert an empty root and the original error.

red.outcome: RED: 2 of 15 tests failed and 13 passed; both partial-stage fault injections left /scadmill residue.

green.outcome: GREEN: 1 test file and all 15 tests passed in 554 ms after best-effort recursive partial-workspace cleanup was added. TypeScript and focused Biome checks also exited 0.

testEvidence.criterion: Output observers cannot break settlement or cleanup, adapter causes remain visible after engine output, and partial stream events retain observed order
testEvidence.test: Inject throwing complete-line and finish-time observers, corrupt STL and summary outputs after warning text, and leave both streams unterminated in reverse lexical order.

red.outcome: RED: 4 of 19 tests failed and 15 passed, reproducing the two Major and one Minor independent-audit findings.

green.outcome: GREEN: 1 test file and all 19 tests passed in 543 ms. TypeScript and focused Biome checks also exited 0 with no further cycles.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
